items.reviewerNotes: Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard shows red 'P' in circle = parking/handbrake indicator. q0534 prompt = 'lights when handbrake pulled up' — direct match. Source options A=Yes/B=No so answer = A.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Source says 'reduce speed in time' on uphill to avoid engine insufficiency. Closest candidate (q0043) requires timely downshift to lower gear — related but not identical (speed vs gear). Insufficient alignment to safely approve an existing qid.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image: rainy follow-ahead vehicle. Source option C = 'ne pas utiliser les feux de route' (do not use main beams) — matches q0497 correct 'cannot use the high beam light' when following another vehicle in rain.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Sign image 'Y002' matches road-number style in q0687. French option C = 'numéro de la voie du district' corresponds to the candidate meaning (township/district road). Minor regional naming risk.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Diamond broken-line = longitudinal (vertical) deceleration marking. q0834 describes vertical deceleration marking. In the French option set that meaning is expressed by option A ('ralentir... bandes verticales'), so return A.

Codex high-risk createNewQuestion: Source image visibly indicates open trunk and French option B says trunk open. Existing q0602/q0606/q0617 candidates have image-answer conflicts for trunk/hood/doors, so do not approve an existing qid; create a new item with key B.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Prompt: vehicle behind when preceding climbs on icy slope. q0174's correct meaning = 'select proper place to stop and climb after vehicle has passed.' That meaning corresponds to source French option A, so approve q0174 and map master correct to source letter A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source claims cars may stop engine and coast downhill (Yes/No). q0037 states sliding in neutral is not allowed (Wrong). Therefore correct source answer is No (B).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: on long downhill, reduce speed to fully use engine braking. q0030 prompt/answer = Right (use lower gear and engine braking). Source Yes/No => answer = A (Yes).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: If oncoming vehicle occupies our lane, safe action is to yield/let them pass. q0120's correct action = yield to them; corresponds to source option B.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source ROW item asks whether heavy braking is allowed with ABS. q0540 has the same ROW semantics (answer = Right/True).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Matches q0133: entering a two-way tunnel requires sidelights or low beams (source option D).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source states you may force an oncoming vehicle to reverse — this is false. q0018 explicitly conveys that forcing the other vehicle is incorrect (Wrong). Rejected top matcher: Top machine candidate was q0075 (U-turn guidance) which does not match this prompt; q0018 in the candidate list better matches the source semantics.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard image clearly shows coolant gauge at 90°C; q0567 matches this ROW item (Right/True).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source states do not change gears while crossing a level crossing to avoid stalling — q0027 has the same guidance (Right/True).

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Image shows an orientation arrow but candidate matches disagree (Y-intersection vs U-turn vs left/right-only). visual semantics ambiguous; cannot safely pick existing qid.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Blue directional sign image and candidate qids conflict (some map to 'straight lane', others to 'lane separation'); signal conflict in matches — keep unresolved for human review.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a mandatory sign with straight and left arrows (allowed straight or left). q0717 maps to 'going straight and left turn' (source option B). Top machine match q0683 conflicted (prohibition wording), so selected q0717. Rejected top matcher: Top candidate q0683 indicated a prohibition ('no going straight and no left turn'), which conflicts with the pictured mandatory blue sign; q0717 better matches the pictured allowed directions.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Road marking photo is ambiguous (multiple painted arrows, one crossed). Candidates disagree on which movement is forbidden. Needs human verification of the exact painted symbols.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks which lights to use when overtaking at night; meaning is 'alternate high and low beams'. q0511's correct meaning is 'use high and low beam lights alternately' — source option A corresponds to that.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Image shows an intersection with lights/crosswalk; source asks if right turn is permitted but visual semantics (signal aspect, signage, pedestrians) are ambiguous. Top machine candidate (q0194) is about speeding through an intersection and is not a semantic match. Rejected top matcher: Top matcher q0194 prompt ('may speed up to go through the intersection') is unrelated to the source question about turning right.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks what to do if there is a traffic jam in an intersection; options include 'stop and wait outside the intersection' which matches q0458's correct meaning. French/source option with that meaning is A.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Prompt asks if the minibus's method of entering the highway is correct. q0260 covers correctness of entering the highway lane; its correctRow = Wrong → French 'No' (B). Image/top candidates align.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source: 'When the position light is turned on, this indicator lights up.' q0555 explicitly: 'It lights when turning on the sidelights (parking lights)' — matches meaning. French 'Yes' = A. Note: top machine candidate q0528 (high beam) conflicts with this candidate. Rejected top matcher: Top matcher q0528 refers to high-beam indicator (conflicts with position/sidelight meaning).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks max speed when crossing a level crossing; q0382's correctOption = 30 km/h. French option C = 30km/h, clear match.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source statement forbids overtaking in tunnels and on steep slopes. q0209 explicitly states overtaking is not allowed in tunnels, steep slopes, or similar sections. Statement is true → French 'Yes' (A).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks what to do if accident has caused injuries. q0181's correct meaning is 'send injured to hospital or make emergency calls', matching French option A.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: when a vehicle breaks down on the highway, passengers must move to safety barrier/emergency lane and contact police. q0210 matches this instruction (true) → French 'Yes' (A).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Manual qbank search finds q0833, the double white broken lines before an intersection meaning slowdown-and-yield line. Source option C is 'slow down and give way to other vehicles'.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard icon shows a person with seatbelt; source asks if lit indicator means 'seat not adjusted'. q0570 states that claim is Wrong. Therefore French answer is 'No' (B). Visual evidence supports seatbelt/open-door style indicator rather than seat-adjust reminder.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Manual qbank search finds q0532, exactly: sudden braking while turning with ABS can skid. Source statement is true; local answer A (Yes). Rejected top q0064 as only broadly icy-road related.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Visual evidence: prohibition sign with upward arrow crossed — means 'no going straight'. Machine top match q0736 has correct = 'no going straight' -> source option C.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Prompt asks legal responsibility when a serious accident constitutes a crime; q0516 matches and lists 'criminal liability' as correct -> source option A.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image: green rectangular motorway sign with '60' advisory panel. Top candidate q0702 identifies this as a recommended speed in poor weather (machine key A) — corresponds to source option B (vitesse maximale conseillée).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: POV overtaking scenario; top candidate q0425 matches prompt 'From which side to overtake?' with correct = left side -> source option C.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement about LOCK ignition position locking the steering wheel matches q0538 (steering wheel locked when key removed in LOCK) -> answer Yes (A).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Bicycle road marking corresponds to q0863, the lane for non-vehicles. French option B ('voie réservée aux véhicules non motorisés') is the closest official meaning.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement 'driver must accelerate when crossing a flooded area' is false; top candidate q0345 corresponds and is Wrong -> source option B (No).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Visual: red octagonal stop sign (Chinese); machine q0854 maps to 'stop to yield' — matches source option C (s'arrêter et céder le passage).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks max permitted speed on highway when visibility <100 m. Top candidate q0411 has prompt matching this exactly and gives 'less than 40 km/h' -> source option A (40 km/h). Candidate scores are not strong but the prompt aligns closely.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source says immediately rescue injured and notify police — matches q0247 (immediate rescue and report). Options are Yes/No so map to French A = Yes / Right.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Prompt: parties may leave after property-damage accident if no dispute — direct match to q0337. French A = Yes/Right.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: forgery/alteration of driving licence is criminal — matches q0265 (criminal liability). Yes = French A.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks which behavior within three years prevents license application; q0446's correct concept is drug injections/use. French option B ('drogués') maps to that concept.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Duplicate of the prior drug-use/license-refusal item. q0446 is the closest existing qid; French option B maps to drug users/drug injections.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: when traffic slow and others merge, polite yielding + keep safe distance — matches q0135 (yield as much as possible). French C corresponds to that meaning.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: muddy roads cause slip/spin risk — matches q0169 (tires can easily spin and skid). French B maps to that choice.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Statement about police giving verbal warning for lending licence to suspended person; candidate matches conflict (some qids say detention/revocation). Conflicting candidate answers and low signal — keep unresolved.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source: night visibility distance shortens — meaning matches q0161 (visibility shorter). French B = 'elle se raccourcit'.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Placement distance and direction for warning triangle on motorway has conflicting candidate guidance (behind vehicle, in-coming direction, opposite lane); source specifies 'opposite lane' creating mismatch — cannot safely map to existing qid.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Source prompt/wording is ambiguous in French (difference between 'acte de contravention' vs 'acte d'infraction'); top automated candidates do not provide a clear exact match (low scores, semantically related but not identical). Cannot confidently map to an existing qid or accept extracted key A.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks which sticker to paste during probationary period; q0488 asks the same and its correct answer is 'probation mark' which corresponds to French option C ('autocollant réglementaire').
